name: Blackeyed Pea Crabmeat Gorditas with Vinaigrette (Pyles)
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups black-eyed peas, soaked overnight and drained
1 quart ham hock broth or water
1 pound fresh Gulf Coast lump crabmeat, shell and cartilage removed
1 medium red bell pepper, seeded and finely diced
1 medium yellow bell pepper, seeded and finely diced
1 medium poblano pepper, seeded and finely diced
1 teaspoon chopped fresh marigold mint or mint to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the Tequila-Orange Vinaigrette by Pyles (See recipe) and set aside.
2. Place the drained black-eyed peas in a large saucepan with the ham hock broth or water. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 45 to 60 minutes or until tender. Toward the end of cooking, taste the peas and season with salt as needed. Drain the peas and set aside.
3. Cook the bacon over medium-high heat in a large skillet until all fat is rendered and crispy brown. Drain on paper towels, then dice. Combine the diced bacon, crabmeat, bell peppers, poblano pepper, onion, scallions, tomato, mint, chervil, and drained peas in a large bowl.
4. Add the Tequila-Orange Vinaigrette to the bowl and stir just to combine. Let stand at room temperature for about 30 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
5. Meanwhile, prepare 8 gorditas (if desired). See the Gorditas (Pyles) recipe.
6. Serve: Split the gorditas open with a small knife. Stuff each with 2 to 3 tablespoons of crabmeat mixture. Alternatively, use two gorditas and fill as you would a sandwich. Garnish with orange sections and cilantro, and drizzle additional vinaigrette on top.
